claim: Separating agent graph architecture from configuration enables deploying multiple specialized agents from a single codebase without code changes
actionable: Extract configurable fields (prompts, models, tools) into a Config class so you can create multiple assistant variants by changing configuration, not code
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Using credits as an abstraction allows you to change which features map to what cost without visible pricing changes to end customers
actionable: When building assistants, use credit abstractions to represent work so you can rebalance feature costs without changing user-facing pricing
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: An assistant is a named, versioned combination of a graph and its configuration that can be deployed, reused, and iterated on independently
actionable: Create named assistants with specific configurations and version them in your agent platform so teams can reference and modify them programmatically
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Visual configuration playgrounds in LangSmith Studio enable non-engineers to create and test specialized agent variants without touching code
actionable: Set up LangSmith Studio with manage-assistant controls so business teams can create and test new assistant configurations through UI
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Node-level configuration using JSON schema extra fields allows multi-agent supervisors to configure different sub-agents independently
actionable: In supervisor architectures, use JSON schema extra to tag configuration fields with the node they belong to, enabling per-node tuning in studio
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Assistant versioning enables safe A/B testing and instant rollback if a new configuration underperforms compared to previous versions
actionable: Deploy new assistant configurations and maintain version history so you can A/B test configurations and roll back to stable versions if needed
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Programmatic assistant management via SDK/API enables CI/CD integration and automation of assistant lifecycle across environments
actionable: Use the LangGraph SDK to create, update, and manage assistants programmatically so you can integrate assistant deployment into your CI/CD pipelines
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents

claim: Threads combined with specific assistant IDs enable users to invoke different agent configurations for the same task without changing calling code
actionable: When invoking agents, pass assistant IDs to route requests to different configurations, allowing users to switch agent behaviors without code changes
source_title: LangGraph Assistants: Building Configurable AI Agents
